title: Fiscal and monetary policy actions in the short run
description: Practice what you have learned about the short-run consequences of using fiscal and monetary policy in conjunction with each other or in opposition to each other.
topic_path: Fiscal and Monetary Policy Actions in the Short Run
Fiscal and monetary policy actions in the short run

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following combinations of monetary policy and fiscal policy would be the appropriate response to a severe recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sell bonds; decrease the reserve requirement
Increase discount rate; decrease government spending

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Isengard is experiencing high inflation and a rate of unemployment below the natural rate.

**Which of the following combination of fiscal and monetary policy actions would be the most appropriate response to this output gap?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Decrease government spending; decrease the money supply
Decrease taxes; increase the money supply
Buy bonds; decrease the reserve ratio

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Mithrim is experiencing a severe recession. As a result, the government is conducting expansionary fiscal policy. The central bank of Mithrim is considering expansionary monetary policy to aid in the recovery efforts. 

**Which of the following is an example of a monetary policy action that would work with the expansionary fiscal policy to close the output gap?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The production possibilities curve (PPC) of the nation of Mithrim is shown here. The economy is currently operating at Point B in the graph below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What combination of monetary and fiscal policy would be the most likely to return the economy to point C?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A bowed out production possibilities curve with "capital goods" on the vertical axis and "consumer goods" on the horizontal axis.  There is a point on the interior of the production possibilities curve labeled "B" and a point on the production possibilities curve labeled "C."

choices.content: Increase the discount rate; increase government spending
Lower the reserve ratio; increase taxes

assessment_items.question_content:
The natural rate of unemployment in Mithrim is 5% and the current rate of unemployment is 9%, 

**Which of the following combinations of monetary and fiscal policy would be appropriate to return an economy to full employment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Increase the discount rate; increase government spending
Lower the reserve ratio; increase taxes

assessment_items.question_content:
The natural rate of unemployment in Mithrim is 5%, and the current rate of unemployment is 2%. Also, the consumer price index (CPI) has been increasing rapidly. 

**Which of the following combinations of monetary and fiscal policy would be appropriate to return this economy to full employment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Increasing taxes; lower the discount rate

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Valinor is currently operating on point X. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**If the central bank sells bonds and the government of Valinor increases taxes, which point in this graph best represents where the economy would be operating as a result of these actions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots one curved line and five labeled points: W, X, Y, Z, and R. The horizontal axis is labeled consumption goods, and the vertical axis is labeled capital goods. The axes are unmarked. A concave production possibilities curve runs from the upper left to the lower right. Points W, X, and Y lie on the curve. Point Z lies above and to the right of the curve. Point R lies below and to the left of the curve.
options.caption: A bowed out production possibilities curve (PPC) with capital goods on the vertical axis and consumption goods on the horizontal axis. There are five points in the image: point R is on the interior of the PPC, Point Y is on the PPC halfway between each axis, Point X is on the PPC to the left of point Y, point W is at the intersection of the PPC on the vertical axis, and Point Z in outside of the PPC.

choices.content: Either point W or point Y

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Rivendell experienced the AD shock shown in this graph. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following combinations of monetary and fiscal policy would be appropriate to combat the effect on the price level shown in this graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: An AD-AS model with price level (PL) on the vertical axis and rGDP on the horizontal axis. An upward sloping curve labeled SRAS, a vertical curve labeled LRAS, a downward sloping curve labeled AD and a downward sloping curve labeled AD2.  The LRAS curve is vertical at a value of rGDP of Yf. The AD1, SRAS, and LRAS curve all intersect at a value of rGDP of Y1 and a value of the price level of PL1. There is an arrow point from the curve labeled AD to the  AD2 curve. The AD2 curve and the SRAS curve intersect at a value of real GDP of Y2 and a value of the price level of PL2.

choices.content: Increase taxes; decrease the reserve requirement
Sell bonds; increase the reserve requirement
Decrease taxes; decrease the reserve requirement

assessment_items.question_content:
The Council of Elrond has decided to increase taxes, and the central bank of Elrond has decided to conduct open market sales of financial assets. 

**Assuming that wages are sticky, which of the following best describes the impact of these actions on real output and the price level?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real output will decrease; the price level will decrease
Impact on real output indeterminate; impact on the price level indeterminate
Real output decreases; impact on the price level is indeterminate
Real output increases; the price level increases
Impact on real output is indeterminate; price level decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
The Council of Elrond has decided to increase taxes, and the central bank of Elrond has decided to conduct open market purchases of financial assets. 

**Assuming that wages are sticky, which of the following best describes the impact of these actions on real output and the price level?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Impact on real output indeterminate; impact on the price level indeterminate
Real output will decrease; the price level will decrease
Real output decreases; impact on the price level is indeterminate
Real output increases; the price level increases
Impact on real output is indeterminate; price level decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
King Thranduil of the Woodland Realm is trying to rescue the kingdom from a severe recession by engaging in expansionary fiscal policy. As a result of the expansionary fiscal policy, the Woodland Realm now has a budget deficit. Meanwhile, the president of the central bank is Smaug, who wants to maintain a steady interest rate. 

**What is the appropriate action for the central bank to take in order to reduce the impact of the expansionary fiscal policy on interest rates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The nation of Eldemar is experiencing rapid inflation, which the government is taking fiscal policy actions to counteract. However, the traitor Ulfang is the chairperson of the central bank of Eldemar and is planning to undermine the government’s efforts. 

**Which of the following is a monetary policy action that Ulfang (as chair of the central bank) could use to block the government’s efforts to control inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
